parameters.query:
SELECT s.token, s.expires_at, s.completed, s.mode, t.id AS tenant_id, t.onboarding_completed
FROM table_3 s
JOIN table_4 t ON t.id = s.tenant_id
WHERE s.token = $1 AND s.completed = false

options.systemMessage:
=You are a business data extractor for Nigerian small businesses. Given a business type, description, and business mode, extract the following and respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no explanation, no preamble.
 
Business modes:
- product_only: sells physical products only
- service_only: offers services or appointments only
- hybrid_product_led: primarily products but also offers services
- hybrid_service_led: primarily services but also sells products
 
{
  "business_category": "one clean category label (e.g. 'Fashion Retail', 'Hair Salon', 'Photography Studio', 'Catering', 'Tailoring')",
  "target_customers": "short phrase describing who buys from table_5 books this business (e.g. 'working women in Lagos', 'event planners and couples', 'small business owners')",
  "unique_selling_point": "one sentence about what makes this business stand out from table_6",
  "key_offerings": ["up to 5 short strings — list the main products OR services OR both depending on business_mode. For service businesses use service names. For product businesses use product categories. For hybrid use both."]
}
 
Rules:
- For service_only and hybrid_service_led: key_offerings should be service names (e.g. 'Hair braiding', 'Bridal makeup', 'Photography sessions')
- For product_only and hybrid_product_led: key_offerings should be product categories (e.g. 'Ankara gowns', 'Ready-made blouses', 'Custom suits')
- Keep all values concise and practical
- Never invent information not present in the input

=You are a catalogue and service parser for Nigerian small businesses. Extract everything useful from table_7 given text. The business mode tells you what kind of content to expect.
 
Business modes:
- product_only: expect product names and prices
- service_only: expect service names, prices/rates, and possibly durations or availability
- hybrid_product_led / hybrid_service_led: expect a mix of both
 
Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no explanation, no preamble:
 
{
  "catalogue_items": [
    {
      "name": "product or service name",
      "price": "price with ₦ symbol, or rate (e.g. '₦15,000', 'From ₦5,000', 'Contact for pricing'), or empty string if not stated",
      "description": "optional short description, duration, or availability note (e.g. '2-hour session', 'available Mon-Sat', 'home delivery included')",
      "item_type": "product OR service"
    }
  ],
  "business_context": "any about-us, background, mission, or description text found — empty string if none",
  "extra_facts": ["short fact strings — delivery info, booking policies, opening hours, FAQs, promotions, contact details, payment terms, cancellation rules, location — anything operationally useful"]
}
 
Rules:
- Always return all four keys in each catalogue_item
- Always return all three top-level keys even if empty
- item_type must be exactly 'product' or 'service'
- For service businesses: capture duration hints (e.g. '1 hour', 'half day') in the description field
- If the text is sparse or unclear, extract what you can and leave other fields as empty strings
- Never invent prices, services, or facts not present in the input
- extra_facts should capture operational detail the agent can use to answer customer questions

value.operating_hours: ={{ JSON.stringify({ open: $('Build Full Config').first().json.open_time, close: $('Build Full Config').first().json.close_time }) }}

parameters.query:
DELETE FROM table_3 
column_6 (SELECT id FROM table_4 column_7 $1) 
  column_4 false;

INSERT INTO table_3 (token, tenant_id, platform, expires_at, completed, mode)
SELECT $2, id, 'telegram', $3::timestamptz, false, $4
FROM table_4
column_7 $1;

parameters.query:
SELECT token, mode
FROM table_3
column_6 $1::uuid
  column_4 false
  column_8 now()
LIMIT 1

Please check your previous message for the link, or tap the button below to open it again.

parameters.query:
SELECT pi.id, pi.access_token, pi.bot_username, pi.bot_webhook_set, pi.is_active
FROM table_13 pi
JOIN table_14 b ON b.id = pi.business_id
WHERE b.tenant_id = $1::uuid
  AND pi.platform = 'telegram'
  AND pi.is_active = true
LIMIT 1

parameters.query:
SELECT b.id AS business_id, b.business_name
FROM table_14 b
WHERE b.tenant_id = $1::uuid
ORDER BY b.updated_at DESC
LIMIT 1

parameters.message: 💡 *Share your idea!*\n\nWhat feature or improvement would make your MySquad even better? Type your suggestion below and send it — we will attend to it. 👇
🚨 *Report a problem*\n\nPlease describe the issue. What happened, when, and what you expected instead. Type below and send. 👇

parameters.query:
SELECT b.id, b.business_name
FROM table_14 b
LEFT JOIN table_13 pi
  ON pi.business_id = b.id
  AND pi.platform = 'telegram'
  AND pi.is_active = true
WHERE b.tenant_id = (
  SELECT id FROM table_4
  column_7 $1
)
AND pi.id IS NULL
ORDER BY b.updated_at DESC
LIMIT 1

nodes.name: Block New Business – Finish Bot First

parameters.query:
SELECT
  bp.id AS business_id,
  bp.business_name,
  pi_wa.access_token AS wa_instance,
  pi_wa.phone_number_id AS wa_number,
  pi_wa.is_active AS wa_active
FROM table_14 bp
JOIN table_4 t ON t.id = bp.tenant_id
LEFT JOIN table_13 pi_wa
  ON pi_wa.business_id = bp.id AND pi_wa.platform = 'whatsapp'
WHERE t.telegram_id = $1

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO table_13 (
  tenant_id, business_id, platform, access_token, phone_number_id, is_active, bot_webhook_set
)
SELECT
  t.id,
  $1::uuid,
  'whatsapp',
  $2,
  $3,
  false,
  true
FROM table_4 t
WHERE t.telegram_id = $4
ON CONFLICT (business_id, platform) DO UPDATE column_10 EXCLUDED.access_token,
  phone_number_id = EXCLUDED.phone_number_id,
  is_active = false

parameters.text: =✅ Your WhatsApp connection is being set up. You'll receive your pairing code here in a moment — just keep this chat open.

parameters.query:
UPDATE table_17
column_11 msq_generate_canonical_name(business_id, COALESCE(category, 'item'))
column_12 $1::uuid AND canonical_name IS NULL
RETURNING id, canonical_name, display_name

SELECT draft_data, last_step
FROM table_3
column_5 $1
  column_4 false
  column_8 now()
LIMIT 1
